title: Elastic beats earnings expectations but its stock crumbles in late trading on mounting losses
text: Elastic beats earnings expectations despite stock selloff in late trading. Q3 FY2026 Earnings Beat: Revenue of $450 million beat expectations. Adjusted earnings per share beat consensus estimates. However, stock declined sharply in late trading session. Market Reaction Disconnect: Despite strong results and raised guidance, stock sold off. Investors focused on growth deceleration from Q2 to Q3. Revenue growth guidance raised but still below historical rates. Detailed Financials: Total revenue $450 million, 18% YoY growth. Sales-led subscription revenue $376 million, 21% YoY growth. Operating margin 18.6%, well above expectations. CRPO $1.056 billion, marking first crossing of $1B milestone. Forward Guidance: Full-year revenue raised to $1.734-$1.736 billion. EPS raised to $2.50-$2.54. Operating margin guidance at 16.3%. Market Sentiment: Investors rewarding companies with growth acceleration, not just absolute growth. ESTC's deceleration from 20%+ to 18% growth perception concerns. Despite strong AI adoption narrative, macro uncertainty weighing on sentiment. Technical Analysis: Share price weakness creating valuation opportunity. Current multiples reflect pessimistic growth assumptions. Catalysts for reversal: AI adoption acceleration, large deal wins, macro improvement.
